skill.name: image-to-video
skill.description: Animate any still image on RunComfy — this skill is a smart router that matches the user's intent to the right i2v model in the RunComfy catalog. Picks HappyHorse 1.0 I2V (Arena #1, native audio, identity preservation) for general animations, Wan 2.7 with `audio_url` for custom-voiceover lip-sync, or Seedance 2.0 Pro for multi-modal animation from image + reference video + reference audio. Bundles each model's documented prompting patterns so the caller gets sharper output without burning iterations on the wrong model. Calls `runcomfy run <vendor>/<model>/image-to-video` (or endpoint variant) through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "image to video", "image-to-video", "i2v", "animate image", "make this move", or any explicit ask to turn a still into video.
skill.summary: Smart router for image-to-video generation on RunComfy. Picks the best i2v model (HappyHorse, Wan 2.7, or Seedance 2.0 Pro) based on user intent — portrait animation, custom-voice lip-sync, or multi-modal composition.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill (SKILL.md) that routes image-to-video requests to the RunComfy CLI. The 117 static findings are false positives: 'backtick execution' matches are markdown code formatting, 'hardcoded URLs' are legitimate RunComfy documentation links, 'path traversal' is example URL syntax, and 'weak crypto' is a false match on the word 'hash'. The skill contains no executable code, no obfuscation, and includes an explicit Security & Privacy section documenting its threat model. Risk is inherent to its function (CLI invocation + network API calls), not to malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Network API calls to external service
low_findings.description: The skill instructs calling the RunComfy CLI which makes HTTPS requests to model-api.runcomfy.net and downloads results from *.runcomfy.net/*.runcomfy.com. This is the core function of the skill (image-to-video generation) and is clearly documented. Users must explicitly install the CLI and authenticate.

low_findings.title: Token storage in user config directory
low_findings.description: runcomfy login writes the API token to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. The skill explicitly documents this behavior and recommends RUNCOMFY_TOKEN env var as a bypass for CI. This is standard practice for CLI tools.

content.user_title: Animate Any Image to Video with AI
content.value_statement: Choosing the wrong image-to-video model wastes iterations and credits. This skill routes your request to the best RunComfy model for your goal — portrait animation, lip-sync, or multi-modal composition — so you get quality output faster.
actual_capabilities: Routes image-to-video requests to HappyHorse 1.0 I2V for portrait and product animation with identity preservation
Routes to Wan 2.7 with audio_url for custom-voiceover lip-sync generation
Routes to Seedance 2.0 Pro for multi-modal animation combining image, video, and audio references
Provides documented prompting patterns for each supported model to improve output quality
Documents CLI invocation syntax including input schema fields and output directory handling
limitations: Picks one model per call — does not blend routes (e.g., HappyHorse animation + Wan lip-sync requires two separate calls)
Inherits each model's duration and resolution limits (15s cap, 720p or 1080p depending on route)
Requires RunComfy CLI installation and authentication before use
Brand-specific model variants not listed (e.g., Wan 2.6) should be handled by their dedicated brand skills

use_cases.title: Social media portrait animation
use_cases.description: Animate a still portrait photo with subtle motion, breathing, and camera drift for Instagram or TikTok posts.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Multilingual marketing videos with lip-sync
use_cases.description: Generate the same talking-head clip in multiple languages by swapping the audio_url while keeping the visual prompt constant.

use_cases.title: Product reveal videos from reference assets
use_cases.description: Combine a product image, a scene reference video, and a brand voice audio into a cohesive animated clip.

prompt_templates.title: Simple portrait animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this portrait with gentle camera drift and subtle breathing motion, keeping identity stable.
prompt_templates.scenario: Default HappyHorse route for any unspecified animation request

prompt_templates.title: Lip-sync to voiceover
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking-head clip from this image, lip-synced to the audio at [URL], medium close-up, soft key light.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user provides an audio track and wants synchronized speech

prompt_templates.title: Multi-modal brand video
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate subject from image 1 walking through the scene in video 1, voice tone from audio 1, 8 seconds duration.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user provides image + video + audio references for Seedance

prompt_templates.title: Product 360 reveal
prompt_templates.prompt: Slow 360 orbit around this product, consistent lighting, packaging unchanged, reveal the back label.
prompt_templates.scenario: E-commerce product showcase using HappyHorse geometry preservation

output_examples.input: User says: 'Animate this headshot with a slight smile and camera drift for my LinkedIn.'
output_examples.output: Routes to HappyHorse 1.0 I2V with a motion prompt like: 'Gentle camera drift, subtle smile, identity-stable features, soft natural light.' Output: 5-second 1080p clip saved to specified output directory.

output_examples.input: User says: 'Make this avatar speak in Spanish' with an English audio file provided.
output_examples.output: Routes to Wan 2.7 with audio_url, generates a talking-head clip with lip-sync to the provided audio. Same image, same prompt, just swap the audio for each language variant.

best_practices: Front-load motion verbs in your prompt (drift, dolly, orbit, tilt) — the model already sees the image, so focus on what changes.
Match duration to audio length when using Wan 2.7 lip-sync — the clip will be silent past the audio endpoint.
Lock the seed parameter when generating multi-language variants to keep visuals consistent across dubs.
anti_patterns: Do not restate the image contents in the prompt — describe only the motion, camera, and lighting changes.
Do not mix radically different aesthetics in multi-modal calls (e.g., watercolor image with photoreal video) — the output will drift.
Do not pass more than one beat per clip — pick a single primary motion (orbit OR dolly OR tilt) for clean output.

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill requires the RunComfy CLI and an authenticated account. Run 'npm i -g @runcomfy/cli' and then 'runcomfy login' to authenticate via browser.

faq.question: Can this skill blend two models in one clip?
faq.answer: No. It picks one model per call. If you need HappyHorse animation plus Wan-style lip-sync, you must make two calls and stitch the results.

faq.question: Where are my generated videos saved?
faq.answer: Videos are downloaded to the path you specify with --output-dir when invoking the CLI. The skill documents this in each route's Invoke section.

faq.question: Is my prompt content sent to RunComfy servers?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompt and input URLs are transmitted to the RunComfy Model API over HTTPS. Treat any external image URLs you pass as untrusted input.

faq.question: What is the maximum video duration?
faq.answer: All three supported models cap at 15 seconds per clip. Wan 2.7 lip-sync is further limited by the audio length (3–30 seconds).

faq.question: Does this skill work with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Install it via 'npx skills add agentspace-so/runcomfy-skills --skill image-to-video'.
